questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
The graph which depicts the results of Rutherford gold foil experiment with α -particles is:
θ: Scattering angle
Y: Number of scattered α -particles detected
(Plots are schematic and not to scale)
questions.solution:
Rutherford carried out an experiment in which he bombarded a thin sheet of gold foil with α-particles and then analysed the trajectory of these particles after they collided with the gold foil.

For a detector at a specific angle with respect to the incident beam, the number of particles per unit area striking the detector is given by the Rutherford formula,

Yθ=NinLZ2k2e44r2KE2sin4θ2

Here, Ni= Number of incident alpha particles,

n= Atoms per unit volume in target,

L= Thickness of target,

Z= Atomic number of target,

e= Electron charge,

k= Coulomb's constant,

r= Distance between target and detector,

KE= Kinetic energy of alpha particles,

θ= Scattering angle.

Therefore, Y∝1sin4θ2
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: Which of the following figure represents the variation of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>ln</mi><mfenced><mfrac><mi>R</mi><msub><mi>R</mi><mn>0</mn></msub></mfrac></mfenced></math> with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>ln</mi><mi>A</mi></math> (if <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi><mo>=</mo></math> radius of a nucleus and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mo>=</mo></math> its mass number)
questions.solution:
We know the relation between radius and atomic mass number is R=R0A13 ⇒lnR=lnR0+13lnA

⇒lnRR0=13lnA 
It is similar to y=mx which is equation of line passing through origin.
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
Given below are two statements

Statement I: In hydrogen atom, the frequency of radiation emitted when an electron jumps from lower energy orbit E1 to higher energy orbit E2, is given as hf=E1-E2

Statement II: The jumping of electron from higher energy orbit E2 to lower energy orbit E1 is associated with frequency of radiation given as f=E2-E1h. This condition is Bohr's frequency condition.

In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below:
options: Both statement I and statement II are true.
Both statement I and statement II are false.
Statement I is correct but statement II is false.
Statement I is incorrect but statement II is true.
questions.solution:
According to Bohr's model, when electron makes the transition from lower orbit to higher orbit, its energy can be given as

 ∆E=Efinal-Einitial=hf, here, h is Planck's constant.

Then, f=E2-E1h.

Bohr's frequency condition is the law that state that the frequency of the radiation emitted or absorbed during the transition of an atomic system between two stationary states equals the difference in the energies of the states divided by Planck's constant.

Hence, Statement I is incorrect but statement II is true.
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
In the given figure, the energy levels of hydrogen atom have been shown along with some transitions marked A,B,C,D and E.
The transitions A,B and C respectively represent
options: The ionization potential of hydrogen, second member of Balmer series and third member of Paschen series.
The series limit of Lyman series, second member of Balmer series and second member of Paschen series.
The series limit of Lyman series, third member of Balmer series and second member of Paschen series.
The first member of the Lyman series, third member of Balmer series and second member of Paschen series.
questions.solution:
A→ Series limit of Lyman series.

B→ Third member of Balmer series.

C→ Second member of Paschen series.
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: In Bohr's atomic model of hydrogen, let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>K</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>P</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>E</mi></math> are the kinetic energy, potential energy and total energy of the electron respectively. Choose the correct option when the electron undergoes transitions to a higher level :
questions.solution:
Total energy of electron is stated as E=-Z2me48nhε02, potential energy P=-Z2me44nhε02 and kinetic energy is given by K=Z2me48nhε02.

So, as electron makes transition to higher level, total energy and potential energy increases due to the negative sign while the kinetic energy of electron decreases.
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.q: Find the ratio of energies of photons produced due to transition of an election of hydrogen atom from its(i) second permitted energy level to the first level, and (ii) the highest permitted energy level to the first permitted level.
questions.solution:
Energy of an electron in the nth orbit of a Hydrogen atom is given by, En=-13.6n2 eV

Therefore,

⇒E2-E1E∞-E1=13.61-1413.6=34
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.q: Two identical photocathodes receive the light of frequencies <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>f</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>f</mi><mn mathvariant="italic">2</mn></msub></math> respectively. If the velocities of the photo-electrons coming out are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>v</mi><mn mathvariant="italic">1</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>v</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> respectively, then
options: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msubsup><mi>v</mi><mn mathvariant="italic">1</mn><mn mathvariant="italic">2</mn></msubsup><mo mathvariant="italic">-</mo><msubsup><mi>v</mi><mn mathvariant="italic">2</mn><mn mathvariant="italic">2</mn></msubsup><mo mathvariant="italic">=</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn mathvariant="italic">2</mn><mo mathvariant="italic"> </mo><mi mathvariant="italic">h</mi></mrow><mrow><mo mathvariant="italic"> </mo><mi mathvariant="italic">m</mi></mrow></mfrac><mstyle mathvariant="italic"><mrow><mo>[</mo><msub><mi>f</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>-</mo><msub><mi>f</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>]</mo></mrow></mstyle></math>

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
A metal exposed to light of wavelength 800 nm and emits photoelectrons with a certain kinetic energy. The maximum kinetic energy of photo-electron doubles when light of wavelength 500 nm is used. The work function of the metal is

(Take hc=1230 eV-nm).
questions.solution:
According to Einstein's photoelectric equation

kmax=hcλ-ϕ

⇒k1=1230800-ϕ       ...1

And ⇒k2=1230500-ϕ

Since, k2=2k1

⇒2k1=1230500-ϕ    ...2

Eliminating k1 from (1) and (2) we get

0=1230500-1230400+ϕ

⇒ϕ=0.615 eV
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A parallel beam of light of wavelength <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>900</mn><mo> </mo><mi>nm</mi></math> and intensity <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>100</mn><msup><mi>Wm</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math> is incident on a surface perpendicular to the beam. The number of photons crossing <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><msup><mrow><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></mrow><mn>2</mn></msup></math> area perpendicular to the beam in one second is
questions.solution:
Wavelength of incident beam λ=900×10-9 m

Energy of a single photon E=hcλ.

Intensity is defined as power per unit area. Therefore,

Intensity of incident beam I=EtA

If the number of photons crossing the given area per second is n, then

n=Enet Esingle pholon =IAλhc

⇒n=1001×10-4900×10-96.62×10-34×3×108=4.5×1016.
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: An <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi></math> particle and a proton are accelerated from rest through the same potential difference. The ratio of linear momenta acquired by above two particals will be :
questions.solution:
For α particle, mα=4 a.m.u and charge qα=2e.

For proton, mp=1 a.m.u and charge qp=e.

As the charged particle are moving through same potential, the kinetic energy acquired by the particle will be equal to electric potential energy i.e., 12mv2=qV, where, V is potential.

Using the relation between momentum p and kinetic energy, we have 

p=2mE=2mqV

Then, the ratio of linear momenta of two particles is pαpp=mαqαmpqp=41×21

=221=22:1
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
In photoelectric effect
A. The photocurrent is proportional to the intensity of the incident radiation.
B. Maximum kinetic energy with which photoelectrons are emitted depends on the intensity of incident light.
C. Max K.E. with which photoelectrons are emitted depends on the frequency of incident light.
D. The emission of photoelectrons require a minimum threshold intensity of incident radiation.
E. Max K.E. of the photoelectrons is independent of the frequency of the incident light.
Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.solution:
With proper energetic radiation, the photoelectric current increases with an increase in accelerating potential until it reaches a point where it reaches its maximum and does not increase with an additional increase in accelerating potential.

For a given potential difference, the photocurrent is directly proportional to the intensity of the incident radiation.

The maximum kinetic energy of the emitted photoelectrons can be written as

Kmax=hν-φ

From above equation, it can be seen that the maximum kinetic energy of the photoelectron depends on the frequency of the light used.

Hence, the statements A and C are correct.
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: The radius <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></math> of a nucleus of mass number <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> can be estimated by the formula <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi><mo>=</mo><mfenced><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>3</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>15</mn></mrow></msup></mrow></mfenced><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>/</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math>. It follows that the mass density of n nucleus is of the order of: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><msub><mi>M</mi><mrow><mi>p</mi><mi>r</mi><mi>o</mi><mi>t</mi></mrow></msub><mo>≅</mo><msub><mi>M</mi><mtext>neut </mtext></msub><mo>≃</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>67</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>27</mn></mrow></msup><mo> </mo><mi>kg</mi></mrow></mfenced></math>
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: Nucleus <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> is having mass number <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>220</mn></math> and its binding energy per nucleon is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>6</mn><mo> </mo><mi>MeV</mi></math>. It splits in two fragments <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>C</mi></math> of mass numbers <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>105</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>115</mn></math>. The binding energy of nucleons in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>C</mi></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>6</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>4</mn><mo> </mo><mi>MeV</mi></math> per nucleon. The energy <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Q</mi></math> released per fission will be:
questions.solution:
The total binding energy of nucleus A is,

EA=220×5.6=1232 MeV

The total binding energy of B and C combined is,

EB+EC=105×6.4+115×6.4=1408 MeV

Therefore, the energy released per fission is,

Q=EB+EC-EA=1408-1232=176 MeV
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: An electron of mass <math><mi>m</mi></math> and magnitude of charge <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>e</mi></math> at rest, gets accelerated by a constant electric field <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>E</mi></math>. The rate of change of de-Broglie wavelength of this electron at a time <math><mi>t</mi></math> is (ignore relativistic effects)
questions.solution:
The acceleration of the electron is

a=eEm

The velocity of the electron as a function of time is

v=at=eEmt

The de-Broglie wavelength is

λ=hmv

λ=hmeEmt

λ=heEt
dλdt=-heEt2
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: The <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Q</mi></math>-value of a nuclear reaction and kinetic energy of the projectile particle, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>K</mi><mi>p</mi></msub></math> are related as
questions.solution:
The Q-value of the reaction is defined as the difference between the sum of the masses of the initial reactants and the sum of the masses of the final products, in energy units.

For any reaction to happen spontaneously there must be some energy released in the process. Therefore,

Kp+Q>0.
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
